We are an agency that specializes in luxury travel.
The prospects who visit our stand get a free brochure.
Patagonia, which is our flagship, attracts thousands of visitors.
Hi, I saw your screen from across the hall. So, what does your company do?
Great question. We are Escape Turismo, an agency that specializes in luxury group travel in South America. Our flagship, which is Patagonia, sells out every season.
Impressive. There are many agencies here. What makes you stand out?
Our selling point is service. We are a well-established company that has a strong track record with corporate groups. Every prospect who books with us gets a dedicated manager.
And the technology? I noticed the screen.
That is our cutting-edge booking platform, where a client can compare packages in real time. Let me showcase it for you. Here is a brochure too.
This is exactly the kind of partner we look for. Our turnover doubled last year, and we need someone reliable.
Then let us talk properly. Take my card, and I will send you the full portfolio, which covers forty destinations, this afternoon.
An agency that specializes in travel.
What makes us stand out.

What makes us stand out is our service.
Every prospect who visits our stand gets a brochure.
Let me showcase our portfolio for you.

Hi, thanks for stopping by. Let me give you the short version. We are Escape Turismo, an agency that specializes in luxury group travel across South America. Our flagship destination, which is Patagonia, sells out every single season. What makes us stand out is service. We are a well-established company that has handled corporate groups for over fifteen years, and every prospect who books with us gets a dedicated manager, someone who is available around the clock. If you like what you hear, take a brochure, and I will send you the full portfolio, which covers forty destinations. It would be great to work together.

Welcome to this short segment on presenting your company. When you describe what you do, relative clauses are your best friend. They let you add detail without starting a new sentence. Use who for people: the team who runs our groups. Use which or that for things: a platform that compares prices. Use where for places: the region where we specialize. Now, here is the key point for a polished pitch. There are two types. A defining clause gives essential information, and it takes no commas: the agency that books your trip. A non-defining clause just adds extra detail, and it takes commas, like this: our flagship, which is Patagonia, sells out fast. And remember, in that second type, never use the word that. Master this, and your pitch will sound clear, confident, and professional.
